# Primary sources

Recheck version-sensitive behavior against the target repository's selected toolchain and locked
dependencies at execution time. When network access is unavailable, report the freshness gap.

- [The Cargo Book: manifest format](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/manifest.html) — package metadata, edition, targets, features, dependencies, and manifest semantics.
- [The Cargo Book: Rust version](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/rust-version.html) — `rust-version` meaning and workspace/MSRV considerations.
- [The Cargo Book: workspaces](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/workspaces.html) — workspace members, inheritance, shared lockfile/target directory, and lint opt-in.
- [The Cargo Book: cargo test](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/commands/cargo-test.html) — target and package selection behavior.
- [The Rust Edition Guide](https://doc.rust-lang.org/edition-guide/) — edition migration and compatibility context.
- [The Rust Book: ownership](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/ch04-00-understanding-ownership.html) — ownership, borrowing, and slices.
- [The Rust Book: error handling](https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/ch09-00-error-handling.html) — recoverable errors and panics.
- [The Rust Reference: unsafe](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/unsafe-keyword.html) — unsafe contexts and operations.
- [The Rust API Guidelines](https://rust-lang.github.io/api-guidelines/) — non-mandatory API review considerations.
- [Clippy documentation](https://doc.rust-lang.org/clippy/) — lint usage, configuration, and applicability.
- [Agent Skills specification](https://agentskills.io/specification) — portable skill structure and progressive disclosure.
- [Codex skills](https://developers.openai.com/codex/build-skills) — current Codex discovery and authoring guidance.
- [Claude Code skills](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills) — current Claude Code skill behavior and format.
- [OpenCode skills](https://opencode.ai/docs/skills/) — current OpenCode skill discovery and compatibility guidance.
